headers.comment: Never cache the HTML shell — it points at the current fingerprinted JS. Without this, a browser/CDN kept serving an OLD index.html (whose old chunks still exist on Vercel, so nothing 404s and the stale-chunk auto-reload never fires), leaving users on stale code after every deploy.
Fingerprinted build assets are immutable — safe to cache forever. This rule is LAST so it wins over the catch-all for /assets/*.
